evals.prompt: I want to do a full account-level cost audit of our AWS account. We're on the bb-east profile and spend mainly on Lambda, DynamoDB, and S3. We have customer and product cost allocation tags on our resources. Our bill has been creeping up and I want to understand where the money is going and find anything we can cut. Can you run through the full audit?
evals.expected_output: Follows the 8-phase audit process. Breaks down costs by service AND by customer/product tags. Identifies which customers and products are driving cost growth. Flags untagged spend. Checks for idle resources, rightsizing, commitments, anomalies. Produces a prioritized report with customer/product attribution.

assertions.text: Verifies AWS credentials/access before starting analysis (aws sts get-caller-identity or equivalent)
Queries Cost Explorer for spend breakdown by service using aws ce get-cost-and-usage with --group-by SERVICE
Breaks down costs by customer tag using --group-by Type=TAG,Key=customer
Breaks down costs by product tag using --group-by Type=TAG,Key=product
Identifies or flags untagged spend (resources missing customer/product tags)
Checks for idle resources: at minimum unattached EBS volumes and unused Elastic IPs
Produces a structured report with cost-by-customer and cost-by-product tables
Report includes prioritized findings with estimated savings amounts
Sets the AWS_PROFILE to bb-east as specified by the user
Covers at least 5 of the 8 phases defined in the skill (scope, spend analysis, idle resources, trusted advisor, rightsizing, commitments, anomalies, report)

evals.prompt: We have a project called 'notifier' deployed in us-east-1. All resources are tagged with Project=notifier and Environment=prod. We also use customer and product tags across our infra. It's a CDK Python project using Lambda, DynamoDB, SQS, and SNS. Can you audit just this project's AWS spending? I think we're overpaying on Lambda memory and might have some orphaned resources from an old deployment.
evals.expected_output: Identifies this as a project-level audit and uses tag-based filtering throughout. Shows cost attribution by customer and product even within the project scope. Checks Lambda memory vs actual usage. Analyzes DynamoDB provisioned capacity utilization. Checks for orphaned CloudFormation stacks, DLQ accumulation, and log retention policies. Validates findings against CDK code.

assertions.text: Correctly identifies this as a project-level (not account-level) audit and uses tag filtering
Filters Cost Explorer queries using --filter with Tags Key=Project Values=notifier
Breaks down project costs by customer and/or product tags to show which customers/products drive this project's spend
Compares Lambda configured memory vs actual peak usage to find over-provisioned functions
Checks DynamoDB provisioned capacity utilization (consumed vs provisioned RCU/WCU) for tables using provisioned mode
Looks for orphaned CloudFormation stacks or individual orphaned resources from old deployments
Checks SQS dead letter queues for message accumulation indicating processing failures
Checks CloudWatch log group retention policies and flags groups with no retention set
Attempts to validate findings against CDK code before final recommendations
Report distinguishes project costs from account-wide costs

evals.prompt: Our AWS bill jumped 40% last month compared to the month before. We use customer and product tags on everything. I need you to investigate what's driving the increase — which customers or products caused it, what services spiked. I'm on the default AWS profile. I need a report I can share with my manager that breaks it down by customer.
evals.expected_output: Investigates the cost spike with customer/product attribution front and center. Shows which customers grew the most, which products drove the increase. Compares MoM by service AND by customer/product. Produces a manager-friendly report with clear customer-level findings.

assertions.text: Compares current month vs previous month spending to identify which services drove the 40% increase
Breaks down the cost increase by customer tag to show which customers drove the spike
Breaks down the cost increase by product tag to show which products drove the spike
Uses daily granularity (DAILY) in Cost Explorer to identify when the spike started
Checks Cost Anomaly Detection (aws ce get-anomalies) for flagged anomalies
Report includes cost-by-customer table showing each customer's contribution to the increase
Produces a report formatted for sharing with management (executive summary, clear findings, recommended actions)
